Preparing Your Appliances for a Smooth Move with Galaxy Movers

While Galaxy Movers handles the heavy lifting and technical aspects of your appliance move, a little preparation on your part can significantly contribute to a smoother and more efficient relocation. Following these simple steps will ensure your appliances are ready for our expert team.

Refrigerators and Freezers: At least 24-48 hours before the move, defrost your refrigerator and freezer. Remove all food items, clean the interior thoroughly, and wipe dry to prevent mold and mildew during transit. Disconnect the water line if you have an ice maker or water dispenser. Keep the doors slightly ajar to allow for air circulation until moving day.

Washing Machines: Disconnect the water supply hoses and drain any residual water from the machine. Secure the drum according to the manufacturer's instructions, often using shipping bolts, to prevent damage during transport. Wrap the hoses and power cord securely.

Dryers (Electric and Gas): Disconnect the power cord and dryer vent hose for electric dryers. For gas dryers, it is crucial to have a qualified professional disconnect the gas line to ensure safety. Clean the lint trap thoroughly. Coil and secure all cords and hoses.

Dishwashers: Disconnect the water supply and drain lines. Remove any food particles and clean the interior. Ensure all racks and components are secured or removed to prevent rattling. It’s important that your dishwasher is completely dry before moving.

Ovens and Stoves: For electric ranges, unplug the unit. For gas ranges, a professional should disconnect the gas line. Remove all racks, grates, and removable parts, packing them separately. Clean the appliance thoroughly to prevent grease and food residue from creating odors or attracting pests during transit.

By taking these preparatory steps, you help our team of Best Appliances Movers in Three Hills ensure a swift and safe relocation of your appliances.
